title: poa tristis subsp. bryophila
intro: Poa tristis subsp. bryophila is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family and is native to the mountainous regions of North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds, derived from its roots and leaves. The health benefits of Poa tristis subsp. bryophila include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to support overall well-being and immune function.
